title: Constraint-Market Compatibility — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing

sections.title: Step 07 — Constraints carried forward from Product Decomposition
sections.content:
9 constraints from §01. No new technology-class constraints added (Step 07 web-research augmentation deferred — operating envelope already exhaustively captured upstream).

| # | Name | Type | is_absolute | Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | false | DSO spare MV capacity required |
| P2 | Land Footprint and Siting | physical | false | 40–80 m²/MWh + clearances |
| P3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | false | VdS / local Feuerwehr; >3 MWh cluster rules |
| P4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | true | NOVEC, compartmentation, deflagration venting |
| P5 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | false | 6–12 months |
| P6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | economic | false | CN LFP concentration; CRMA/EU Battery Reg |
| P7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | false | −20 to +50 °C |
| P8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | true | 10–15% (thermodynamic floor) |
| P9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | false | €250–400/kWh installed |

P4 and P8 are flagged `is_absolute=true` at the commodity level (physics), but neither is a *market-side* knockout against 334413 — the fab environment never forces the product beyond those limits; they are mitigated in design.

---

sections.title: Step 08 — Per-Market Assessment against 334413
sections.content:
### Summary table

| # | Constraint | Verdict | Cost | Time | Core rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | **mitigable** | medium | 6–12 mo | Fab has its own HV/MV substation; BESS ties into internal MV bus, not DSO feeder. Load-flow + breaker coordination study required. |
| P2 | Land Footprint and Siting | **mitigable** | low | 2–4 mo | Fab sites are large industrial parcels (e.g. Infineon Dresden, ESMC Dresden ≫50 ha); utility-yard placement standard. Fire clearances drive siting, not total area. |
| P3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | **mitigable** | medium | 3–6 mo | Fab insurance regime (FM Global / VdS) stricter than default VdS; BESS container requires separation-distance study, often upgraded suppression, and GC sign-off. |
| P4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | **mitigable** | medium | 6–12 mo | Fab-grade risk review (FM Global DS 5-33 equivalent), deflagration-vented compartments, gas-detect interlocks, potentially remote-sited container. Standard in fab-power engineering. |
| P5 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | **none** | — | — | Fab greenfield build cycles are 24–36 months; 6–12 mo VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 cert runs in parallel with cleanroom fit-out. Not on critical path. |
| P6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | **mitigable** | medium | ongoing | Fab owners (Infineon, Bosch, ESMC) bound by EU Chips Act + sovereignty optics — may require EU cell content or dual-sourcing. Mein Solar substitutes EU/KR cells at 5–15% cost premium. |
| P7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | **none** | — | — | German fab sites (Dresden, Reutlingen, Magdeburg) within −20 to +50 °C envelope. HVAC inside container handles balance. |
| P8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | **none** | — | — | Fab use-case is ride-through + PQ, not arbitrage. Roundtrip efficiency immaterial when energy-service is millisecond-to-second ride-through. For PV+BESS self-consumption add-on, 10–15% loss is accepted as cost of flex. |
| P9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | **mitigable** | high | 12–18 mo | Fab business case is *availability* not arbitrage: ride-through saves one lost batch = €M–€10M+, so €/kWh economics invert. Sale must reframe from arbitrage-payback to availability-insurance + Netzentgelte + decarbonization stack. Not a cost block, a narrative block. |

### Knockout analysis

No knockouts. Fab environment is *inside* the product's operating envelope for every physical/chemical constraint. Every regulatory / economic barrier has a known certification or engineering path.

**Cross-check against knockout false-positive rules:**
- No cost-based knockout (P9 correctly mitigable — cost is never knockout).
- No performance-degradation knockout (P8 correctly none — no zero-output condition in 334413).
- No regulatory-without-path knockout (P3, P4, P6 all have certification / engineering paths).
- No physics gap (ambient fab utility-yard conditions are benign; temperature, acoustic, footprint envelopes comfortably met).

### Conservative review (08b simulation)

Applied two-question test to every mitigable verdict. No verdicts upgraded to knockout (validator only downgrades). None downgraded from mitigable to none (we were already conservative: P1, P3, P4, P6, P9 are genuine engineering/commercial work).

---

sections.title: Surviving / eliminated
sections.content:
| Status | NAICS | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `markets_surviving` | **334413** | Zero knockouts. Enters Step 09 (feature-market fit) and Step 12 (prioritization) with mitigation cost overhead: 1×high, 4×medium, 1×low. |
| `markets_eliminated` | — | — |

**Cumulative mitigation cost load (this market):**
- 1× high (P9 — business-case reframing)
- 4× medium (P1, P3, P4, P6)
- 1× low (P2)
- Time to full readiness: **12–18 months** (longest single item P9; P4 and P1 parallelizable inside).

---

sections.title: Strategic notes (for downstream scoring)
sections.content:
1. **The binding constraint is not technical, it's commercial.** P9 dominates: fab owners don't buy on €/kWh; they buy on availability-insurance + decarbonization. Mein Solar sales motion must pivot, not re-engineer.
2. **P4 cost is conservative.** Fab fire reviews (FM Global) are stricter than VdS. Container may need to sit outside fab perimeter, increasing cable runs — but still well inside "medium" bucket.
3. **P5 is a timing gift, not a barrier.** Fab 24–36 mo build cycles absorb VDE-AR-N 4110 lead time completely.
4. **P6 links to sovereignty narrative.** EU Chips Act fab owners likely *prefer* non-CN cells; this could become a differentiator rather than a penalty.
5. **Consistent with VN strategic read:** entry mode recommendation (subcontract under M+W / Exyte / Jacobs, lead with PV+BESS) maps directly onto the mitigation stack — all P-items collapse to the GC's existing engineering ledger.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
Upstream only (no web search):
- `01_product_decomposition.md` — constraints P1–P9 definitions, thresholds, severity
- `02_functional_promise.md` — FP Extension axes and required complements (P5, P6, P9 context)
- `05_market_discovery.md` — architecture distance 6, MEDIUM priority, emerging adoption for 334413
- `VN_semiconductor_and_related_device_manufac.md` — fab utility-yard L6H-POWER positioning, SEMI F47 context, FM Global insurance regime, fab build-cycle durations, sovereignty / EU-cell preference

rows.Name: Fire-Protection Clearance
rows.Threshold: VdS / local Feuerwehr; >3 MWh cluster rules

rows.Name: Battery Supply Chain Concentration
rows.Threshold: CN LFP concentration; CRMA/EU Battery Reg

rows.Core rationale: Fab has its own HV/MV substation; BESS ties into internal MV bus, not DSO feeder. Load-flow + breaker coordination study required.
Fab sites are large industrial parcels (e.g. Infineon Dresden, ESMC Dresden ≫50 ha); utility-yard placement standard. Fire clearances drive siting, not total area.
Fab insurance regime (FM Global / VdS) stricter than default VdS; BESS container requires separation-distance study, often upgraded suppression, and GC sign-off.
Fab-grade risk review (FM Global DS 5-33 equivalent), deflagration-vented compartments, gas-detect interlocks, potentially remote-sited container. Standard in fab-power engineering.
Fab greenfield build cycles are 24–36 months; 6–12 mo VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 cert runs in parallel with cleanroom fit-out. Not on critical path.
Fab owners (Infineon, Bosch, ESMC) bound by EU Chips Act + sovereignty optics — may require EU cell content or dual-sourcing. Mein Solar substitutes EU/KR cells at 5–15% cost premium.
German fab sites (Dresden, Reutlingen, Magdeburg) within −20 to +50 °C envelope. HVAC inside container handles balance.

Fab use-case is ride-through + PQ, not arbitrage. Roundtrip efficiency immaterial when energy-service is millisecond-to-second ride-through. For PV+BESS self-consumption add-on, 10–15% loss is accepted as cost of flex.

Fab business case is *availability* not arbitrage: ride-through saves one lost batch = €M–€10M+, so €/kWh economics invert. Sale must reframe from arbitrage-payback to availability-insurance + Netzentgelte + decarbonization stack. Not a cost block, a narrative block.

rows.Rationale: Zero knockouts. Enters Step 09 (feature-market fit) and Step 12 (prioritization) with mitigation cost overhead: 1×high, 4×medium, 1×low.
